question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorContinue() handler in the Flux pipeline of downloadAndParseSigFiles(), which silently swallows errors from individual node signature downloads, to cause the mirror node to proceed with an incomplete set of signatures that may not represent true network consensus, allowing a tampered record file to reach the consensus threshold with fewer valid signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CutoverService.get() callback mechanism in download() to inject a malicious RecordFile via getLastRecordFile() that sets lastStreamFile to a file with an attacker-controlled hash value, causing all subsequent verifyHashChain() calls to accept record files whose previousHash matches the attacker-injected hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the streamFile.getType().isChained() check in verifyHashChain() by serving a record file whose StreamType reports isChained() as false, causing the entire hash chain verification to be bypassed and allowing the file to be accepted regardless of its previousHash value, breaking the integrity of the Hashgraph history chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a record file where streamFile.getMetadataHash() returns null and the corresponding signature's getMetadataHashAsHex() also returns null, causing verifyHash() to pass via Objects.equals(null, null), allowing a record file with no metadata hash to be accepted as valid and stored in Hashgraph history without metadata integrity verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file whose getSidecars() list contains sidecar entries with types that match acceptedTypes, but the actual sidecar file served contains records of different types that also pass the second filter in downloadSidecars() because acceptedTypes is empty, allowing injection of arbitrary sidecar record types into Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a sidecar file that causes sidecarFileReader.read() to populate sidecar.getActualHash() with a byte array of the same length as sidecar.getHash() but with different content, and exploit a timing side-channel in Arrays.equals() to confirm hash values and iteratively craft a collision, ultimately bypassing the sidecar hash check and injecting tampered contract data into Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verifySignatures() calls streamFileProvider.get(dataFilename).block() without validating the dataFilename against the expected filename format, allowing a path traversal or filename manipulation in getDataFilename() to cause the mirror node to download and process a tampered record file from an unexpected storage location?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with a very large number of sidecar entries in getSidecars(), causing the Flux.fromIterable() pipeline in downloadSidecars() to make an unbounded number of streamFileProvider.get() calls, exhausting connection pool resources and causing a denial of service that prevents legitimate Hashgraph history from being ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Utility.archiveFile() call in getSidecar() when downloaderProperties.isWriteFiles() is true, by crafting a sidecar filename via StreamFilename.from(recordFilename, sidecar.getName()) that contains path traversal sequences, causing the archive operation to write attacker-controlled bytes to arbitrary filesystem locations and potentially overwrite legitimate Hashgraph history files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Utility.archiveFile() call in verifySignatures() when downloaderProperties.isWriteFiles() is true, by crafting a record filename that resolves to a path traversal via importerProperties.getArchiveDestinationFolderPath(), causing legitimate archived Hashgraph history files to be overwritten with tampered content?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timeout() operator in the Flux pipeline of downloadAndParseSigFiles() by deliberately delaying responses from compromised nodes, causing legitimate node responses to time out and leaving only attacker-controlled node signatures in the sigFilesMap, which may then reach the consensus threshold with tampered hashes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionSidecarRecord with a consensusTimestamp that does not exist in the record file's items, causing records.containsKey(timestamp) to return false for all legitimate items but the attacker's timestamp to be silently ignored, while a different attacker-controlled timestamp matches a legitimate item and injects falsified sidecar data into that transaction's history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the broad Exception catch block in verifySignatures() that logs errors and continues to the next signature, allowing a crafted record file that throws an unexpected exception during streamFileReader.read() to be silently skipped, causing the mirror node to fall back to a different node's copy of the file which may be tampered?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Scheduled annotation on download() with a fixed delay derived from recordDownloaderProperties.getFrequency(), by causing the download cycle to be artificially delayed through resource exhaustion, creating a window where the lastStreamFile state is stale and a tampered record file can be injected as the next file in the chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the contentEquals() comparison in verifyHashChain() between streamFile.getPreviousHash() and expectedPreviousHash, where a Unicode normalization difference or encoding inconsistency between the two strings causes contentEquals() to return false for a legitimate file, causing the mirror node to reject valid Hashgraph history and accept a tampered replacement?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ArrayListMultimap used in downloadSidecars() to inject multiple sidecar records with the same consensusTimestamp, causing recordItem.setSidecarRecords() to associate a list containing both legitimate and attacker-controlled sidecar records with a transaction, corrupting the Hashgraph history for that transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a sidecar file where sidecar.getHash() returns null and sidecar.getActualHash() also returns null after sidecarFileReader.read(), causing Arrays.equals(null, null) to return true and bypassing the hash integrity check, allowing a completely unverified sidecar file to be accepted and its records injected into Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file where recordFile.getSidecars() returns an empty list, causing downloadSidecars() to return early without processing any sidecars, and then serve a different version of the same record file with non-empty sidecars to a different mirror node instance, creating an inconsistency in the Hashgraph history across mirror node replicas?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verifySignatures() only calls onVerified() for the first CONSENSUS_REACHED signature that successfully downloads and verifies a data file, and if that node's data file is tampered but passes hash verification (due to a compromised node key), the tampered file is accepted as valid Hashgraph history without checking other nodes' copies?
